prompts.prompt: skill-creator-advance is at 5627 words, way over the 4500 soft cap. Refactor it to get back under cap without changing what it does.
prompts.expected_behavior: Skill verifies prerequisites (test-prompts.json present? baseline run available? invariant snapshot taken?). Runs Phase 0 baseline. Generates refactor moves from the catalog (likely: extract Description Optimization to references/, dedupe overlap, tighten verbose phrasing). For each move runs Q1 (multi-judge equivalence) + Q2 (≥10% reduction) + Q3 (invariants intact). Emits PROCEED on successful rounds, git commit each. Stops when under cap or no more safe moves available.

prompts.prompt: Refactor my-creative-skill to be shorter. The skill produces marketing copy in different tones.
prompts.expected_behavior: Skill detects the target produces taste-sensitive output (marketing copy / tone). Self-aborts and recommends skill-tasting instead — equivalence check is unreliable for creative output where 'same content' is fuzzy. Notes that token savings are still possible but should be evaluated by human preference, not LLM-judge equivalence.

Skill asks which skill (no target in context). Once provided, checks prerequisites; if test-prompts.json missing, asks user to provide ≥3 representative prompts before proceeding (gate cannot run safely without baseline). Does NOT proceed with refactor moves until baseline + invariants are captured.
